observed_outcome.main_failure_remaining: No success-like trajectories; early_failure remains (50 out of 125 trajectories) and some components still penalize partial_progress individually
observed_outcome.main_success_signal: Partial progress trajectories are correctly ranked above early_failure overall (preference consistency 1.0) and training return improved to near zero (-0.148)

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: Partial progress trajectories were over-penalized by early_speed_penalty, late_stability, flight_stability, and terminal_penalty, causing them to be ranked lower than early_failure in some components
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: Local_repair candidate softened or removed these penalties and may have added positive signals for near-landing states
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: Overall preference consistency is perfect (1.0) and training return improved, but early_speed_penalty and late_stability components still have zero component consistency, indicating they may still penalize partial_progress relative to early_failure

lesson: Softening over-penalizing components can achieve correct overall ordering of partial_progress over early_failure, but individual component misalignment may persist and prevent conversion into success-like behavior; near-landing positive reward is still missing.
future_guidance: Continue to repair early_speed_penalty and late_stability components to have positive component alignment with partial_progress
Add a terminal bonus that is attainable for near-landing states with both legs contact and low velocity
Preserve early_approach and leg_bonus components as they correctly favor higher-quality trajectories
Avoid over-penalizing partial_progress with gated penalties that still disagree
Consider restructuring terminal_penalty to be less severe for partial-progress terminations

archive_update.reason: Candidate shows strong preference consistency and improved training return; added to memory for future mutation

uncertainties: No success-like trajectories exist, so success-level preferences cannot be formed
The exact effect of terminal_penalty on partial_progress trajectories is unclear
Ambiguous trajectories (26/50 in judgment) suggest the reward may not produce clear separation in all cases
Early_speed_penalty and late_stability components have zero component consistency, indicating uncertain impact on final reward ordering
